metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `_validate_routing_entry` needs to be updated to explicitly validate `require_parent_id` as a boolean (it currently silently passes any unknown applies_at key), and if so, add that to scope; update the plan's premise to reflect that the validator already silently accepts unknown axes.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `validate_packaged_routing()` and 'the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis' `require_parent_id`, implying the validator currently rejects or needs updating to accommodate it. In reality, `_validate_routing_entry` in `overlay.py` (lines 190-212) does NOT enforce a closed set on `applies_at` keys — it only rejects two legacy keys (`levels`, `container_only`) and validates `scope` values; any other key silently passes through. So `require_parent_id` would already be silently accepted with no changes. This ambiguity means the plan neither clearly scopes the work (if validation should be extended to type-check the new key's boolean value) nor accurately represents the current validator behavior.
findings.location: What section — 'ROUTING' / 'validate_packaged_routing() and the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis'
findings.suggested_fix: Add explicit boolean validation for `require_parent_id` in `_validate_routing_entry` (analogous to how `scope` is validated), and restate the plan's premise as 'extend the validator to type-check the new axis' rather than 'make it accept' (which already happens).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml` to the Scope as an edit target, with a `- prompt: plan-review-G7 / when: ${{ steps.assemble.outputs.include_G7 }}` entry in the `criteria:` batch block.
findings.finding: The plan does not enumerate `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml` as an edit target, but this file contains the static `criteria:` batch list (one entry per built-in criterion with `prompt: plan-review-<ID>` and `when: ${{ steps.assemble.outputs.include_<ID> }}`). Adding G7 to CANONICAL_LLM, the routing JSON, and the assemble step will emit an `include_G7` boolean, but without a matching entry in the YAML's `criteria:` block the G7 finder will never be dispatched to the batch. All 28 existing built-in criteria have explicit YAML entries (lines 55-117); G7 needs one too.
findings.location: Scope section / src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml (lines 55-117)
findings.suggested_fix:
Add to the `criteria:` list in plan-review.yaml (in alphabetical/logical order among the G-series entries):
```yaml
- prompt: plan-review-G7
  when: '${{ steps.assemble.outputs.include_G7 }}'
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml` to the Scope for threading `ancestor_plan: ${{ steps.verify_inputs.outputs.ancestor_plan }}` in the `with:` block of BOTH verifier steps (the agentic arm and the single-turn arm).
findings.finding: The plan says `plan_review_verify_inputs` (workflow_ops.py:250) will emit a new `ancestor_plan` value that the verifier prompts consume via a `{{parent_plan}}/ancestor_plan` slot, but the gate YAML wires the verifier's `with:` inputs explicitly (e.g. `plan: ${{ steps.verify_inputs.outputs.plan }}`). The new `ancestor_plan` output needs to be threaded in the YAML's `with:` block at both verify call sites (lines 148-150 and ~240-250 of the YAML), but the gate YAML is not listed in the Scope as a target for this change.
findings.location: CONTEXT ASSEMBLY / SURFACING section; src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml lines 136-155 and ~225-250

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the surfacing seam: G7's parent plan context must be available to the Pass-1 AGENT finder (e.g. as `extra_context` injected into `pass1_chunk` for G7, or relied upon via the AGENT's own tool loop calling `show_ticket(parent_id)`) — not exclusively in the Pass-2 verifier prompt; also thread `ancestor_plan` into the verifier for re-grounding, but not as the sole injection point.
findings.finding: The plan misidentifies the seam where G7's parent-plan context must be injected: it says 'G7 runs in the verify pass, whose context is emitted by plan_review_verify_inputs' and proposes adding {{ancestor_plan}} to the verifier prompt — but G7 is AGENT-tier and runs as a Pass-1 agentic finder (like G6/E4/G1G2), not in Pass-2. The verifier re-grounds already-found findings; it does not surface new ones. If ancestor_plan is injected only into the verifier prompt, the G7 finder runs without the parent plan and cannot detect scope drift — it produces no findings because it never sees the parent's text.
findings.location: What section — 'SURFACING (precise seam, verified against the code): G7 runs in the verify pass…'
findings.suggested_fix: Since G7 is exec:AGENT, the simplest mechanically-correct path is: (1) the G7 rubric prompt instructs the agentic finder to call show_ticket(ctx.state['parent_id']) itself (tool access is already granted in agentic mode), making the PlanContext injection optional for the finder; (2) still emit ancestor_plan from plan_review_verify_inputs so the verifier can re-ground G7 findings against the parent plan. If injection is preferred over self-service reads, inject ancestor_plan as extra_context in pass1_chunk for the G7 criterion chunk (like the G5 decomposition_state_block pattern), not via the verifier prompt.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the Pass-1 injection seam for the parent plan: either (a) instruct the G7 rubric to call `show_ticket(parent_id)` directly (making the verifier injection unnecessary), or (b) identify how the pre-assembled `parent_plan` blob is fed into the G7 agent's system prompt at Pass-1 (the ISF/_ticket_graph_blob pattern), not the Pass-2 verifier prompt.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that injecting `{{parent_plan}}` into the Pass-2 verifier prompt and adding `parent_plan` to `PlanContext` is the correct seam for G7 (an `exec: AGENT` criterion) to receive the parent's plan text. This is architecturally inconsistent: AGENT-tier criteria run as autonomous tool-using loops in Pass-1 (see pass1.py's agent fan-out), not in Pass-2. The verifier prompt's `{{parent_plan}}` slot is consumed by the Pass-2 verifier (which confirms findings), not by the G7 agent (which generates them). `PlanContext` fields are not automatically injected into an AGENT criterion's prompt context — the existing AGENT criteria (G1G2, E4, A1, T1) fetch context via tool calls within their own rubric prompts. The plan does not identify the actual Pass-1 seam for pre-assembling parent context into the G7 agent's prompt (analogous to how `_ticket_graph_blob` is injected into ISF's system prompt at pass1.py). This is an unverified capability assertion about how G7 receives its parent-plan context.
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY (NEW mechanism — named edit targets)' and 'SURFACING (precise seam, verified against the code)'
findings.suggested_fix: Pattern (a) is the path of least resistance: G7's rubric prompt instructs the agent to call `show_ticket(ctx.state['parent_id'])` to read the parent's description — this requires no new PlanContext field and no verifier-prompt change. The bounded char-cap logic can live in the rubric itself ('read parent description, truncate to first 2000 chars'). The `PlanContext.parent_plan` field and verifier `{{parent_plan}}` slot can then be dropped from scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Commit to a single slot name (`ancestor_plan`) and specify the rendering mechanism — either a second `resolve_prompt` variable alongside `plan`, or injection into the verifier `instructions` field — so the parent plan text is actually reachable inside the verifier prompt.
findings.finding: The `{{ancestor_plan}}` (or `{{parent_plan}}`) slot name for the verifier prompt is unresolved: the plan offers two alternative names (`ancestor_plan` vs leaving `pass1 var` untouched) without committing to one, and the rendering mechanism — how a second template variable reaches the verifier prompt when `_resolve_system` currently bakes only `{{plan}}` into the system prompt — is left unspecified.
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY (NEW mechanism)' / 'NAMING NOTE'; workflow_ops.py plan_review_verify_inputs; passes.py _resolve_system

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the G7 Pass-1 agent finder receives the parent plan text — either via `extra_context` (like G5's decomposition block), as a new `RunRequest` field, or via the agent's own tool call to the ticket store — and update `pass1_chunk` or a G7-specific finder path accordingly.
findings.finding: The Pass-1 G7 agent finder has no specified mechanism for reading the parent plan: `pass1_chunk(agentic=True)` renders only the leaf's own `{{plan}}` via `_resolve_system`, and the plan only specifies surfacing `ancestor_plan` at the verify step — leaving the finder unable to detect leaf-exceeds-parent drift at Pass-1.
findings.location: What section — 'SURFACING (precise seam)'; passes.py pass1_chunk / _resolve_system; orchestrator.py route_criteria

findings.finding: The mechanism by which `parent_plan` reaches the G7 AGENT criterion's context is structurally underspecified. The plan states it goes through 'the AGENT criterion-context assembly in plan_review/workflow_ops.py (the plan_review_assemble_criteria / grounding step)' — but `plan_review_assemble_criteria` emits only `include_<ID>` booleans and a routing record (no per-criterion context), and no step in `workflow_ops.py` currently builds AGENT-criterion-level context payloads. The agentic batch runner reads the ticket plan from `PlanContext.plan_text`, not a per-criterion context dict. How `PlanContext.parent_plan` actually flows into the G7 agent's system prompt or instructions is not specified.
findings.location: What section: 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY (NEW mechanism — named edit targets)' and 'SURFACING (corrected — G7 is an AGENT criterion...)'; Scope: 'plan_review/workflow_ops.py (the plan_review_assemble_criteria / grounding step that builds each AGENT criterion's context from PlanContext)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the parent-realign move definition to the plan: specify its move id (e.g., '13'), `name` (e.g., 'realign to parent'), `template` string encoding the parent-wins rule and the realign-or-update-parent-first instruction, and its `applies_when: ['G7']` filter.
findings.finding: The new parent-realign Pass-4 coach move is referenced in scope ('the coach MOVE_REGISTRY (one parent-realign move in passes.py)') but its move id, `name`, `template` string, and optional `applies_when` filter are never defined anywhere in the plan.
findings.location: Scope section — 'the coach MOVE_REGISTRY (one parent-realign move in passes.py)'; passes.py MOVE_REGISTRY lines 729-792
findings.suggested_fix: "13": {"name": "realign to parent", "template": "Realign {subject} to the parent ticket's declared scope — or update the parent first if the parent's scope is genuinely wrong — before re-reviewing this leaf.", "applies_when": ["G7"]}

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify adding `has_parent_id: bool` as a property to `PlanContext` (derived from `bool(self.state.get('parent_id'))`) and add it as a parameter to `applies()`, mirroring how `has_children` is handled today.
findings.finding: `has_parent_id` is used as the routing gate for G7 in `route_criteria()` / `applies()`, but `PlanContext` has no `has_parent_id` field or property today, and the plan says PlanContext 'already knows the parent chain' — which is incorrect for the current dataclass.
findings.location: What section — 'ROUTING'; det_floor.py PlanContext lines 94-133; registry.py applies() lines 311-339
findings.suggested_fix: Add `@property def has_parent_id(self) -> bool: return bool(self.state.get('parent_id'))` to PlanContext; add `has_parent_id: bool = False` to `applies()` signature and use it to gate `require_parent_id: true` entries.

findings.finding: The plan asserts that the `applies_at` schema validator (`_validate_routing_entry` in `overlay.py`) will need to "accept" the new `require_parent_id` axis, but the actual validator at overlay.py:190-212 silently ignores all unrecognized `applies_at` keys (it only validates `scope` and rejects legacy keys `levels`/`container_only`). This means a misspelled or misconfigured `require_parent_id` value will be silently ignored rather than caught at load time, and `applies()` won't enforce it unless `registry.py`'s `applies()` is also updated to read it — which requires an explicit, coordinated edit to BOTH `overlay.py` (add validation) and `registry.py:applies()` (add the enforcement branch).
findings.location: What section / Scope section — 'validate_packaged_routing() and the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis'; overlay.py:131-212; registry.py:311-339
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: 'extend `_validate_routing_entry` in `criteria/overlay.py` to validate `require_parent_id` as a boolean when present in `applies_at`' and ensure the Scope already calls out the `registry.py:applies()` extension explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the naming-note: the `parent_plan` kwarg at pass1.py:118 is a Python function argument to `pass1_container()` (the container/G3-G4 path) that renders into the `{{plan}}` template slot — not a prompt variable named `parent_plan` in the leaf finder path; clarify which prompt/path the naming collision actually affects.
findings.finding: The plan states 'pass1.py:118 already binds a prompt var literally named `parent_plan` to `ctx.plan_text` (the ticket's OWN plan, in the finder path)'. Reading the code: line 118 passes `parent_plan=ctx.plan_text` as a Python keyword argument to `passes.pass1_container()`, which renders it into the `{{plan}}` template slot of the `plan-review-container` prompt — NOT a template variable literally named `parent_plan`. Furthermore, this is in the **container** path (G3/G4 criteria), not 'the finder path' (the leaf finder `pass1_chunk` uses a parameter named `plan`, not `parent_plan`). The naming-collision concern the plan raises is real, but the description of the existing code is inaccurate on both the variable name and the code path.
findings.location: What section — 'NAMING NOTE: pass1.py:118 already binds a prompt var literally named `parent_plan`'

findings.finding: The plan asserts G7 should be registered in `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` (registry.py:66), but `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` is the set that triggers agentic (tool-using) Pass-2 verification specifically for criteria that reason about the live CODE repo (E4/G1G2/A1/G6). G7 reasons about the TICKET STORE (parent ticket content), not the code repo. Adding G7 to CODEBASE_GROUNDED would cause any G7 finding to trigger the more expensive agentic verifier, but the verifier's code-grounding tooling is mismatched to re-grounding parent-ticket containment findings.
findings.location: What section — 'register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM/AGENT_TIER/CODEBASE_GROUNDED'; registry.py:66 (`CODEBASE_GROUNDED`); workflow_ops.py:215-232 (`plan_review_grounding` step)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py` to the Scope as an edit target (specifically `_validate_routing_entry` at line 131), since that is where `applies_at` schema validation is actually enforced, not in `registry.py`.
findings.finding: The plan says to update `validate_packaged_routing()` and the `applies_at` schema validator in `registry.py` to accept the new `require_parent_id` axis, but the actual `_validate_routing_entry` function that validates `applies_at` schema entries lives in `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py` (at line 131), not in `registry.py`. `registry.py:188` only re-exports it as an alias. If the implementer edits only `registry.py`, the upstream validator in `overlay.py` will remain unmodified and may reject — or silently ignore — the new `require_parent_id` key depending on its strictness.
findings.location: Scope section / What section ('validate_packaged_routing() and the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis') / src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py:131

findings.finding: `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py` — specifically `_validate_routing_entry` — is the function that actually validates `applies_at` fields in the packaged routing schema. The plan says 'the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis' and names `validate_packaged_routing()` as the gating check, but `_validate_routing_entry` (overlay.py:131–212) is the code that parses `applies_at` and currently only checks `scope`, `suppress_types`, `suppress_when`, and two legacy fields. Adding `require_parent_id` to criteria_routing.json without updating this validator means `validate_packaged_routing()` will silently accept (or silently ignore) the new key — no validation of its value type (bool). This file is not named as an edit target in Scope.
findings.location: Scope section / src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py:131-212 (_validate_routing_entry)
findings.suggested_fix: In `_validate_routing_entry` (overlay.py), after the `scope` check, add: `rpi = ap.get('require_parent_id'); if rpi is not None and not isinstance(rpi, bool): raise CriteriaError(...)`

findings.finding: The plan states 'validate_packaged_routing() and the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis,' but the applies_at sub-key validator (_validate_routing_entry in src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py:131-212) is NOT listed as a named edit target in the Scope section. Currently, _validate_routing_entry only validates scope values and rejects two legacy keys; any unknown applies_at key (including require_parent_id) is silently ignored rather than validated. Without updating overlay.py, the new axis would pass validation undetected, making the Scope-listed 'validate_packaged_routing()+applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis' claim incomplete.
findings.location: Scope section / What section ('validate_packaged_routing() and the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis')
findings.suggested_fix: In overlay.py's _validate_routing_entry, after validating `scope`, add: `if 'require_parent_id' in ap and not isinstance(ap['require_parent_id'], bool): raise CriteriaError(...)`. Also add it to the Scope section alongside registry.py.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Choose and commit to a single file path for the G7 promotion-path documentation and state it explicitly in the plan (e.g., 'docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md'), removing the 'or' alternative.
findings.finding: The file path for the in-session promotion-path doc is expressed as an either/or ('docs/plan-review-gate.md or a new docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md'), leaving the actual location unresolved. AC4 states the file must be CI-verifiable (implying a path-existence check), but a CI assertion cannot verify two alternative paths simultaneously.
findings.location: What section — POSTURE paragraph; Acceptance Criteria AC4
findings.suggested_fix: If docs/plan-review-gate.md already exists, append a ## G7 Promotion Path section there and state that path. If it does not exist, commit to docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md as a new file. Either way, remove the 'or' and state the chosen path in both the What and AC4 sections.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Commit to a single canonical name for the parent-plan context field throughout (PlanContext attribute, verify-side emission variable, and prompt slot) and state it explicitly in the plan — e.g., 'the field is named `ancestor_plan` everywhere: PlanContext.ancestor_plan, the emitted value, and the {{ancestor_plan}} prompt slot'.
findings.finding: The name of the new context field injected into the verify path is expressed as a choice ('named `ancestor_plan` (or the pass1 var is left untouched and only the verify path gains the new slot)'), leaving the actual field name as an either/or the executor must resolve. The plan also uses 'parent_plan' for the PlanContext field in det_floor.py and 'ancestor_plan' for the verify-side slot, creating two different names for what may be the same value. An executor implementing this must invent a canonical name without a stated default.
findings.location: What section — CONTEXT ASSEMBLY paragraph; Scope section — 'det_floor.py PlanContext (add `parent_plan` field)' vs. 'plan_review_verify_inputs emits the ancestor_plan value'
findings.suggested_fix: Pick `ancestor_plan` throughout and update the Scope and What sections to use it consistently: `det_floor.PlanContext.ancestor_plan`, `plan_review_verify_inputs` emits `ancestor_plan`, the verifier prompt slot is `{{ancestor_plan}}`. Remove the parenthetical 'or' clause.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the naming inconsistency between What and Scope: pick one name (`ancestor_plan` is the plan's stated preference) and use it exclusively throughout the Scope section's edit-target list, removing the ambiguous '{{parent_plan}}/ancestor_plan' phrasing.
findings.finding: The plan resolves the `parent_plan` naming collision in the What section by choosing `ancestor_plan` for the verify-path field, but the Scope section re-introduces ambiguity by writing '{{parent_plan}}/ancestor_plan slot G7 reads' — using both names interchangeably. An implementer reading Scope in isolation could use `parent_plan` for the new slot, colliding with the existing pass1 binding that the What section explicitly flags as already taken.
findings.location: What section (NAMING NOTE) vs. Scope section ('new {{parent_plan}}/ancestor_plan slot G7 reads')
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, replace '{{parent_plan}}/ancestor_plan slot G7 reads' with '{{ancestor_plan}} slot G7 reads' to match the What section's stated resolution.

findings.finding: The `require_parent_id` boolean axis is added to the `applies_at` schema but the plan never specifies the validation rule the schema validator (`_validate_routing_entry` in `overlay.py:131`) must enforce for it. The plan says 'validate_packaged_routing() and the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis' but `_validate_routing_entry` currently validates `applies_at.scope` explicitly (non-empty list of 'container'/'leaf') and silently ignores unknown keys. The plan's intent ('must accept') is ambiguous: does it mean 'do not reject' (current behavior, already works) or 'validate the value is boolean' (new logic required)?
findings.location: What section: 'ROUTING ... validate_packaged_routing() and the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis'; Scope: 'registry.py (add the require_parent_id axis to the applies_at schema + applies())'
findings.suggested_fix: In `_validate_routing_entry`, after the existing `scope` validation block, add: `if 'require_parent_id' in ap and not isinstance(ap['require_parent_id'], bool): raise CriteriaError(f'{where}: criterion {cid!r} applies_at.require_parent_id must be a boolean')`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fixture test covering a truncated-parent scenario (parent plan > 2000 chars) to verify G7 either fires correctly on the truncated text or the truncation marker is visible enough that the rubric can flag uncertainty, de-risking the asserted 2000-char cap before promotion.
findings.finding: The 2000-char-per-ancestor cap is asserted without derivation or a specific probe to validate it. There is no planned measurement of whether 2000 chars conveys enough parent context for G7 to produce reliable containment judgements (e.g., a parent plan that is dense or structured may truncate the key ACs).
findings.location: What section: 'BOUNDED via a CHAR CAP of 2000 chars per ancestor ticket'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add test cases for the context-assembly boundary conditions: (a) parent ticket with empty/missing description returns a safe fallback (not an error), (b) unresolvable parent_id is handled gracefully (log + empty ancestor_plan, not an exception), and (c) parent plan text at/over the 2000-char cap is truncated correctly with the ellipsis marker.
findings.finding: The test plan covers the three semantic outcomes (contained/out-of-scope/contradicting) and routing, but omits boundary scenarios for the context-assembly path: (a) parent ticket exists in the store but has an empty/no description, (b) parent_id exists but the parent ticket cannot be read from the store (deleted/inaccessible), and (c) the truncation boundary (parent plan text exactly at or just over the 2000-char cap).
findings.location: AC line 4 ('The parent's plan text is injected … with a test asserting the assembly'); Scope 'tests … a test asserting parent_plan context assembly'; What section — CONTEXT ASSEMBLY char-cap paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: In the context-assembly tests, add three fixture variants alongside the happy-path: `parent_empty_desc`, `parent_not_found` (store returns None), and `parent_oversize` (2001-char description). Assert that `PlanContext.ancestor_plan` is `''`, `''`, and `description[:2000] + '…'` respectively, with no exception raised.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify graceful-degradation for the parent-chain store read in `_assemble_context_uncached`: define what happens when the parent is missing or the read fails (skip G7 for this run and log a warning? emit an advisory 'context unavailable' finding?), add a log/metric for the empty/truncated parent_plan case, and ensure the G7 rubric states explicitly that an empty ancestor_plan should result in criterion skip, not a spurious finding.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new store read in `orchestrator._assemble_context_uncached` to populate `PlanContext.parent_plan`, but specifies no error-handling or graceful-degradation behavior when the parent ticket cannot be retrieved (missing, deleted, or store transient failure). The routing gate `require_parent_id: true` only checks that `parent_id` is present on the leaf ticket's metadata — it does not confirm the parent record exists in the store. If the store read fails or returns nothing, the plan is silent on whether G7 should skip, surface an error, or proceed with empty parent context (risking a hallucinated 'no findings' or a hard crash in context assembly).
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY (NEW mechanism)' / orchestrator.py `_assemble_context_uncached` / AC: 'The parent's plan text is injected into the criterion context'
findings.suggested_fix: In `_assemble_context_uncached`, wrap the parent chain read in a try/except; on failure or missing parent, set `parent_plan` to `None` and log a warning with the ticket ID. In `route_criteria()` (or in G7's `applies()` gate), add a secondary check: if `parent_plan` is None/empty after the store read, suppress G7 for this run rather than executing it against empty context. Add a counter metric `plan_review.g7.parent_plan_unavailable` so operators can detect data-consistency issues.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale for choosing context injection over agent self-service reads (or, if self-service reads are the correct path, drop the PlanContext.parent_plan injection and rely on the AGENT's tool loop with a bounded char cap inside the rubric instead).
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for why the parent plan must be injected into context (via PlanContext + verify-slot) rather than having the G7 AGENT finder read the parent ticket itself via the tool loop (show_ticket is available to all agentic criteria). Since exec:AGENT grants tool access, the agent can self-service the parent plan — which is exactly how G6/E4/G1G2/A1 read codebase context. The absence of this rationale leaves the implementer without justification for the added complexity of PlanContext.parent_plan + the new verify-slot.
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY (NEW mechanism — named edit targets)' and 'SURFACING' subsections

findings.finding: The plan specifies a bounded char-cap truncation for parent/grandparent plan text but provides no test asserting the truncation boundary — the test suite can go green with truncation logic that is silently broken (e.g., off-by-one, no-op), allowing oversized context to reach the LLM or empty context to reach the criterion.
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY (NEW mechanism)' / AC4 — 'a test asserting the assembly'
findings.suggested_fix: Include a fixture with a synthetic parent description longer than the cap and assert `len(context.parent_plan) <= CHAR_CAP` (or equivalent) in the context-assembly test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback for parent ticket read failure: a broad-except (matching the children pattern) that logs a warning, sets `parent_plan` to empty/None, and causes G7 to abstain (PASS not-applicable) rather than crash or fire spuriously.
findings.finding: The fallback for a parent ticket read failure during `parent_plan` assembly in `_assemble_context_uncached` is unspecified: the plan says to populate the field by 'reading the parent chain from the store' but gives no error-handling rule (skip G7 / use empty string / fail the review).
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY'; orchestrator.py _assemble_context_uncached lines 163-211

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `parent_plan` vs `ancestor_plan` naming ambiguity: specify the exact attribute name on `PlanContext` and confirm that `plan_review_verify_inputs` reads that same attribute name when emitting the `ancestor_plan` prompt variable.
findings.finding: The plan uses two different names for the same PlanContext field (`parent_plan` in the `det_floor.PlanContext` declaration, `ancestor_plan` on the verify side) without specifying which name the field actually carries on `PlanContext` itself, creating implementation ambiguity that could cause attribute errors at the seam between `_assemble_context_uncached` and `plan_review_verify_inputs`.
findings.location: What section — CONTEXT ASSEMBLY paragraph; NAMING NOTE; Scope list item 'det_floor.py PlanContext (add `parent_plan` field)'
findings.suggested_fix: Pick ONE canonical name for the `PlanContext` field (recommend `ancestor_plan` to avoid any confusion with pass1's existing `parent_plan` local) and use it consistently in `det_floor.py`, `_assemble_context_uncached`, and `plan_review_verify_inputs`. Update the Scope bullet to say `ancestor_plan` throughout.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether G7's routing (skip when no parent_id) is better handled as a one-line early-continue in `route_criteria()` (mirroring the ISF special-case at orchestrator.py:349) rather than a new `require_parent_id` axis on the `applies_at` schema, to avoid expanding the shared validator and `applies()` signature for a single-criterion need.
findings.finding: The `require_parent_id` boolean axis on `applies_at` is a premature abstraction (Rule-of-Three: only G7 uses it) that could be replaced by a one-line route-time check in `route_criteria()`, avoiding schema/validator changes in the shared `rebar.llm.criteria.overlay._validate_routing_entry`.
findings.location: Scope section / registry.py applies_at schema / orchestrator.py:route_criteria / criteria/overlay.py:_validate_routing_entry
findings.suggested_fix: In `route_criteria()`, add before the `registry.applies()` call: `if c.get('id') == 'G7' and not ctx.state.get('parent_id'): continue`. Register G7 in `criteria_routing.json` with only `applies_at: {scope: ["leaf"], suppress_types: ["bug","session_log"]}` (no new axis). This is the same pattern as the ISF early-continue and requires zero changes to `applies()`, `_validate_routing_entry`, or the overlay schema.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the parent plan text should be fetched lazily (inside G7's AGENT runner, or via a `plan_review_verify_inputs`-level read, analogous to ISF's `_linked_session_log`) rather than eagerly in `_assemble_context_uncached` for every ticket review.
findings.finding: Adding `parent_plan` to `PlanContext` in `_assemble_context_uncached` introduces an eager `show_ticket` store read for every ticket review, including containers (which G7 never runs on) and leaf tickets with no parent — all for a field only G7 reads.
findings.location: What section / det_floor.py:PlanContext / orchestrator.py:_assemble_context_uncached

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion (or extend AC2) requiring at least one integration-level test that exercises the full G7 execution path end-to-end — either against the live LLM or a realistic recorded response — confirming that: (a) the ancestor_plan context is non-empty in the verifier prompt, and (b) a structured G7 finding is returned for a known-divergent fixture pair.
findings.finding: G7 has `exec: AGENT` (it calls the LLM) and ships at advisory posture — meaning it fires on every qualifying leaf-with-parent review in production, not behind a non-default opt-in flag. The ACs require fixture-driven tests for routing and semantics, but no AC explicitly requires an end-to-end exercise of the live G7 execution path (i.e., an actual plan-review invocation on a real or realistic ticket that causes G7 to run and return a structured finding). Fixture tests that mock the LLM call can pass while the live code path has a wiring bug (e.g., the ancestor_plan slot is populated but not rendered into the verifier prompt, or the AGENT execution fails silently).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1, AC2, AC5; What section — 'exec: AGENT'; POSTURE paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'An integration test (may use a VCR/recorded response fixture rather than a live call if CI budget is constrained) verifies that when G7 runs on a divergent leaf+parent fixture pair, the verifier prompt rendered to the model contains a non-empty ancestor_plan value and the returned finding has criterion_id G7.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state that `require_parent_id` is an OPTIONAL field in the `applies_at` schema with a default of `false`, and verify that `validate_packaged_routing()` treats its absence in existing entries as equivalent to `false` (no required migration of existing criteria entries).
findings.finding: The plan adds a new `require_parent_id` boolean axis to the `applies_at` schema and says `validate_packaged_routing()` must accept it, but does not specify the default value when the key is absent from existing criteria entries in criteria_routing.json. If the updated validator treats an absent key as an error rather than defaulting to `false`, all existing criteria definitions break at validation time.
findings.location: What section (ROUTING — MECHANISM); Scope section (registry.py applies_at schema)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope or What section, add: 'require_parent_id is optional; absent = false; existing criteria_routing.json entries require no change.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an update to ADR-0036 (or a new section in docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md) documenting the `require_parent_id` routing axis as an architectural decision and capturing the `ancestor_plan` vs `parent_plan` naming rationale as a stable, committed artifact.
findings.finding: The new `require_parent_id` boolean axis added to the applies_at schema is an architectural extension to the criteria-routing contract, but no update to ADR-0036 (or a new ADR/design-doc section) is planned to capture this decision. The naming-collision resolution (ancestor_plan vs parent_plan) is also a stable contract decision that appears only in plan prose.
findings.location: Scope section: registry.py applies_at schema + NAMING NOTE in What section
findings.suggested_fix: Append a 'Routing axes' section to docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md (already a regeneration target) that defines require_parent_id, its schema contract, and the ancestor_plan naming convention with a cross-reference to the pass1.py collision note.

findings.finding: The plan says G7 receives `parent_plan` 'through the AGENT criterion-context assembly in `plan_review/workflow_ops.py` (the `plan_review_assemble_criteria` / grounding step that builds each AGENT criterion's context from PlanContext).' But `plan_review_assemble_criteria` (workflow_ops.py:144-199) only emits `include_<ID>` boolean routing flags — it does NOT build per-criterion context. AGENT criteria assemble their own context via their tool-using agent loop at execution time; there is no 'grounding step that builds each AGENT criterion's context from PlanContext' in the current workflow_ops.py. This is a false description of the current mechanism that would mislead implementation.
findings.location: What section — 'SURFACING (corrected — G7 is an AGENT criterion, NOT the pass1 finder): G7 receives parent_plan through the AGENT criterion-context assembly in plan_review/workflow_ops.py (the plan_review_assemble_criteria / grounding step that builds each AGENT criterion's context from PlanContext)'

findings.finding: The plan names `plan_review_assemble_criteria` and `plan_review_grounding` in `workflow_ops.py` as the seam where G7 receives `parent_plan` context, but these ops do not build per-criterion finder context — `plan_review_assemble_criteria` emits only `include_<ID>` routing booleans, and `plan_review_grounding` computes only the `code_grounded` boolean from findings. The actual injection path for pre-resolved context to an AGENT criterion's finder call is `pass1_chunk`'s `extra_context` parameter (passes.py:281-345), already used for G5's decomposition state block injection via `run_pass1`'s `_chunk` closure (pass1.py:411-421). The stated mechanism — 'populating `parent_plan` via `plan_review_assemble_criteria`/grounding' — does not exist and would leave G7's finder without the parent plan text.
findings.location: What section — 'SURFACING (corrected — G7 is an AGENT criterion...)' paragraph; Scope — 'plan_review/workflow_ops.py (surface parent_plan into G7's AGENT criterion context via plan_review_assemble_criteria/grounding)'
findings.suggested_fix: In `run_pass1` (pass1.py), read `ctx.parent_plan` (the new `PlanContext` field) and pass it as `extra_context` only for the chunk whose criteria include G7 — mirroring the G5 pattern: `extra = (ctx.parent_plan or decomp_context) if any(c.get('id') == 'G7' for c in chunk) else decomp_context`. Update the Scope/What sections to name `pass1.py`/`run_pass1` as the injection site, not `workflow_ops.py`.

findings.finding: The char cap for parent and grandparent plan-text injection is never quantified. The plan says 'truncate each to a char cap' and 'bounded to parent + grandparent' but states no concrete number, forcing the executor to invent a limit. AC5 ('bounded to parent + grandparent, with a test asserting the assembly') cannot be verified without knowing what 'bounded' means, because the test fixture must encode the expected truncation boundary.
findings.location: What section — CONTEXT ASSEMBLY paragraph: 'BOUNDED (truncate each to a char cap; no whole-subtree dumps)'; AC5: 'bounded to parent + grandparent'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the CONTEXT ASSEMBLY paragraph: 'Cap each level at 2 000 characters (constant `PARENT_PLAN_CHAR_CAP = 2000` in det_floor.py); if the text exceeds the cap, truncate at the last whitespace boundary before the limit.' Update AC5 to read: '…bounded to 2 000 chars per level, with a test asserting assembly truncates at the cap boundary.'

findings.finding: The `parent_plan` field's char-cap (the truncation bound applied to parent + grandparent title/description before injection) is referenced throughout the plan as 'BOUNDED (truncate each to a char cap)' but the actual cap value is never specified anywhere in the plan. An executor implementing `PlanContext.parent_plan` population must invent the number.
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY (NEW mechanism)' / 'BOUNDED (truncate each to a char cap; no whole-subtree dumps)'

findings.finding: The char cap for parent_plan truncation is never specified — neither in the What section ('truncate each to a char cap') nor in AC5 ('bounded to parent + grandparent') — so the end-state-observable proof for the bounding behavior is incomplete (E3) and no boundary test can verify correct truncation of an oversized parent description (E5).
findings.location: What section (CONTEXT ASSEMBLY), AC5, tests spec ('a test asserting the assembly')
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section change 'truncate each to a char cap' to 'truncate each level to N chars (suggested: 2 000 chars per ancestor level)'; update AC5 to 'bounded to ≤ N chars per ancestor level'; add a test: given a parent description of length > N, assert PlanContext.parent_plan contains at most N chars for that level.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
